mnemonics: WIRES - Waste Isn't Really, Everyone Segregates. (Waste Generation & Storage, Collection, Transfer & Transport, Processing & Recovery, Disposal, Education & Monitoring)
keyPoints: Waste Generation: The creation of waste materials at the source.
Collection: The gathering and transportation of waste from the source to a processing or disposal site.
Processing & Recovery: The treatment of waste to recover resources (e.g., recycling, composting) or reduce its volume/hazard.
Disposal: The final placement of waste in a landfill or other designated facility.
Education & Monitoring: Public awareness programs and systems to track the efficiency and environmental impact of the SWM process.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on disposal (like landfilling) and ignoring the crucial upstream elements like source reduction, recycling, and recovery.
Assuming collection is the same as processing; collection is just the transportation, while processing involves changing the waste's form (e.g., at a MRF).
Overlooking the importance of the 'Education' element, which is critical for the success of the entire system (e.g., public buy-in for segregation).
